title: Versioning, Delete Markers, Object Lock, and Restore · Spiral Review
summary: A delayed handoff reconstructs versioning, delete markers, object lock, and restore from browser, Node, TypeScript, AWS SDK v3, S3, CloudFront, security, billing, and stakeholder evidence and challenges one compile-time misconception. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for immutable-retention and recovery runbook after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: In a strict Node 24 and TypeScript 7 service, distinguish unversioned, enabled, and suspended versioning and test null versions, overwrite behavior, and lifecycle impact.
In a strict Node 24 and TypeScript 7 service, list versions and delete markers, resolve current reads, restore by removing a marker or copying a version, and preserve audit identity.
In a strict Node 24 and TypeScript 7 service, apply governance, compliance, legal hold, retention date, and authorized bypass by version with irreversible-risk review.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: S3, CloudFront, and TypeScript Versioning State Transition · changed case · Review Versioning State…
steps.instruction:
review case — A review TypeScript file-delivery team handles case 4482 during a private media entitlement change. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to narrow versioning state and require version-specific retention and restore evidence; retain keyboard and screen-reader progress, cancellation, error, and recovery evidence; then double concurrent Upload instances and preserve aggregate memory and worker budgets. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. Node and AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, PostgreSQL, credentials, host state, handle and stream checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: In a strict Node 24 and TypeScript 7 service, distinguish unversioned, enabled, and suspended versioning and test null versions, overwrite behavior, and lifecycle impact.

Which action demonstrates Versioning State Transition without dropping earlier contracts?

Evidence target: S3, CloudFront, and TypeScript Versioning State Transition · changed case · Review Versioning State….

Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to narrow versioning state and require version-specific retention and restore evidence; bound bytes, time, attempts, promises, streams, concurrency, cache, and spend; then abort during a stream or multipart promise and prove every task and part reaches a terminal state. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. Node and AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, PostgreSQL, credentials, host state, handle and stream checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. lines.label: risk
lines.text: A documented restore command proves recovery without executing a drill. Type annotations, generated SDK shapes, and resolved promises do not strengthen that runtime claim.

lines.label: evidence

options.text: Apply the visible technique immediately, omit assumptions, and treat one successful output as complete proof.

The record says the result looks right but supplies no quantity, trace, condition, or counterexample. That claim does not survive the separate evidence from confidence evidence boundary.
